metadata.title: Reproduction shortlist with validated extension studies

selection_metadata.title: Probes & steering reproduction shortlist
selection_metadata.objective: Pick reproduction targets for a clean, scalable pipeline that runs probes — then steering vectors (incl. persona) — across many model families and sizes, reproducing each paper's original result before extending to new models. Probes are prioritized first per mentor guidance.
scope_filters: Has a probing or steering signal in the source corpus (SAE-only / circuit-only excluded).
Sits in an LLM/NLP context (drops vision/self-supervised 'linear probing' used as an evaluation protocol).
Not a self-supervised / vision-transfer eval paper unless it carries a genuine interpretability signal.
Text modality only — audio / speech / EEG / ECG / biosignal papers are dropped.

relevance_score.how: Each paper is matched against a term taxonomy over title + abstract + keywords. Strong unambiguous terms (e.g. 'steering vector', 'linear probe', 'mechanistic interpretability') weight 3, medium terms 2, weak/context terms 1, with a +1 bonus when a term appears in the title. A paper is kept only if it hits a strong term or an explicit interpretability keyword and totals at least 3.

composite_score.formula: focus + reproducibility + cross_model + impact + recency + topic, capped at 100

components.focus: Probe/steering alignment from the corpus category scores; probing weighted 1.0 vs steering 0.85 ('probes first').
components.reproducibility: Reusable code to build the pipeline on. {'repo': 22, 'promised': 12, 'unknown': 6} points for repo link / code promised / none detected (from abstract or fields).
components.cross_model: Cross-model readiness: model families (up to 14 pts for >=4 families) plus a gradual parameter-size span (8 pts when max/min >= 4x, e.g. 1B->70B).
components.topic: Safety/alignment incentive: 2 pts per distinct safety/alignment term (<=10) + 4 for persona steering, capped at 14.

field_notes.families/sizes: Parsed from the abstract as a cross-model-readiness signal; verify per paper.
field_notes.code_status: repo = link found, promised = code release mentioned, unknown = none detected (absence is not proof there is no repo).
field_notes.citations: Google Scholar count from the conference dump; null when unavailable.

link_validation.method: arXiv links checked against the arXiv API (existence + title-match to flag wrong/hallucinated ids); other links checked over HTTP. Extensions whose link resolves to a different paper (title mismatch) are removed.

papers.title: AxBench: Steering LLMs? Even Simple Baselines Outperform Sparse Autoencoders

extensions.title: SAEs Are Good for Steering -- If You Select the Right Features
extensions.evidence: Directly responds to AxBench's finding that SAEs underperform by proposing output-score feature selection that yields 2-3 times steering improvements, explicitly citing AxBench in its motivation (abstract and intro).

link_check.arxiv_title: SAEs Are Good for Steering -- If You Select the Right Features

extensions.title: Does higher interpretability imply better utility? A Pairwise Analysis on Sparse Autoencoders
extensions.evidence: Cites AxBench and SAEBench to frame its own pairwise analysis of SAE utility vs. interpretability trade-offs, engaging the benchmark's methodology.

link_check.arxiv_title: Does higher interpretability imply better utility? A Pairwise Analysis on Sparse Autoencoders

extensions.title: Enhancing LLM Steering through Sparse Autoencoder-Based Vector Refinement
extensions.evidence: Cites AxBench in its references and proposes SAE-based refinement as a direct response to AxBench's negative result on SAE steering performance.

link_check.arxiv_title: Enhancing LLM Steering through Sparse Autoencoder-Based Vector Refinement

papers.search_notes: Searched arXiv, Semantic Scholar, Google Scholar. AxBench arXiv ID: 2501.17148. Several papers explicitly engage AxBench's benchmark findings. Most extensions arrived after ICML 2025 acceptance.

papers.title: DISCO: Disentangled Communication Steering for Large Language Models
papers.search_notes: Searched OpenReview (NeurIPS 2025 forum id c8AjdgdHnD), arXiv, Google Scholar. No qualifying follow-up papers found; paper appears very recent (NeurIPS 2025) and citation indices have not caught up.

papers.title: Detecting Strategic Deception with Linear Probes

extensions.title: The Secret Agenda: LLMs Strategically Lie and Our Current Safety Tools Are Blind
extensions.venue: arXiv preprint / AAAI 2026 Workshop AIGOV submission
extensions.evidence: Extends the Apollo deception-probe methodology to SAE probes, explicitly reproducing the linear-probe baseline and testing its generality vs. SAE probes, concluding SAEs are 'unlikely to be a magic bullet' in this setting.

link_check.arxiv_title: The Secret Agenda: LLMs Strategically Lie and Our Current Safety Tools Are Blind

papers.search_notes: Searched arXiv (2502.03407), Semantic Scholar, Apollo Research blog, LessWrong. ICML 2025 poster. Found one direct follow-up via a community literature review gist. arXiv link needs verification against paper availability.

papers.title: Detecting High-Stakes Interactions with Activation Probes
papers.search_notes: Searched OpenReview (NeurIPS 2025 forum id 8YniJnJQ0P), arXiv, Google Scholar. Paper is from NeurIPS 2025 and is very recent; no genuine follow-ups found.

papers.title: Improving Instruction-Following in Language Models through Activation Steering

extensions.title: Enhancing Instruction Following of LLMs via Activation Steering with Dynamic Rejection
extensions.evidence: Builds directly on Stolfo et al. (2025)'s instruction-following activation steering framework, adding dynamic rejection to address oversteer failures; cites the paper in motivation and experimental comparison.

link_check.arxiv_title: Enhancing Instruction Following of LLMs via Activation Steering with Dynamic Rejection

extensions.title: Prompt-Activation Duality: Improving Activation Steering via Attention-Level Interventions
extensions.evidence: Explicitly lists Stolfo et al. (2024/2025) instruction-following steering as the line of work it builds upon, extending steering to attention-level interventions for KV-cache-aware control.

link_check.arxiv_title: Prompt-Activation Duality: Improving Activation Steering via Attention-Level Interventions

extensions.title: Steering Code LLMs with Activation Directions for Language and Library Control
extensions.evidence: Applies the instruction-following activation steering paradigm from Stolfo et al. to code-generation LLMs for programming language and library control, citing the original paper in methodology.

link_check.arxiv_title: Steering Code LLMs with Activation Directions for Language and Library Control

extensions.title: Denoising Concept Vectors with Sparse Autoencoders for Improved Language Model Steering
extensions.evidence: Cites Stolfo et al. in its baseline comparison of activation steering methods for instruction following, testing whether SAE-denoised vectors outperform plain difference-in-means steering.

link_check.arxiv_title: Denoising Concept Vectors with Sparse Autoencoders for Improved Language Model Steering

papers.search_notes: Searched arXiv (2410.12877), Microsoft Research page, OpenReview (wozhdnRCtw), Google Scholar. Has 4 OpenAlex citations; additional web-search pass found several direct methodological extensions.

papers.title: Angular Steering: Behavior Control via Rotation in Activation Space
papers.search_notes: Searched OpenReview (NeurIPS 2025 forum id dGi2d5yDs4), arXiv, Google Scholar. Paper is NeurIPS 2025; no qualifying follow-ups found in this web pass.

papers.title: Programming Refusal with Conditional Activation Steering

extensions.title: C-ΔΘ: Circuit-Restricted Weight Arithmetic for Selective Refusal
extensions.evidence: Lists CAST (Lee et al., ICLR 2025) as reference [2] and directly contrasts its circuit-restricted weight-arithmetic approach against conditional activation steering for selective refusal control.

link_check.arxiv_title: $C$-$ΔΘ$: Circuit-Restricted Weight Arithmetic for Selective Refusal

papers.search_notes: Searched arXiv (2409.05907), IBM GitHub repo, OpenReview (Oi47wc10sm), Google Scholar. ICLR 2025 Spotlight paper. Found one direct follow-up in a refusal-steering extension paper.

papers.title: SCANS: Mitigating the Exaggerated Safety for LLMs via Safety-Conscious Activation Steering
papers.search_notes: Searched arXiv (2408.11491), AAAI OJS, GitHub (zouyingcao/SCANS), Semantic Scholar. Found one medium-confidence extension via ResearchGate snippet citing SCANS in related work on conditional refusal behavior.

papers.title: LayerNavigator: Finding Promising Intervention Layers for Efficient Activation Steering in Large Language Models
papers.search_notes: Searched OpenReview (NeurIPS 2025 forum id wj4lM45xQR), arXiv, Google Scholar. Very recent NeurIPS 2025 paper; no qualifying follow-ups found.

papers.title: Towards Interpretability Without Sacrifice: Faithful Dense Layer Decomposition with Mixture of Decoders
papers.search_notes: Searched OpenReview (NeurIPS 2025 forum id jcvX8XFNqX), arXiv, Google Scholar. Very recent NeurIPS 2025 paper; no qualifying follow-ups found.

papers.title: REAL: Reading Out Transformer Activations for Precise Localization in Language Model Steering
papers.search_notes: Searched OpenReview (ICLR 2026 forum id P38RYdkFLI), arXiv, Google Scholar. ICLR 2026 paper; no qualifying follow-ups found, as expected for a paper this recent.

papers.title: The Linear Representation Hypothesis and the Geometry of Large Language Models

extensions.title: The Geometry of Categorical and Hierarchical Concepts in Large Language Models
extensions.venue: ICLR 2025 (oral), Best Paper ICML 2024 Workshop on MI
extensions.evidence: Directly extends Park et al. (2024)'s binary-concept formalization to categorical and hierarchical concepts, authored by overlapping authors, and proves new geometric results about polytope representations.

link_check.arxiv_title: The Geometry of Categorical and Hierarchical Concepts in Large Language Models

extensions.title: Toward a Flexible Framework for Linear Representation Hypothesis Using Maximum Likelihood Estimation
extensions.evidence: Explicitly extends Park et al. (2024) by removing the single-token-pair constraint via MLE on von Mises-Fisher distributions, demonstrated on LLaMA models across diverse concepts.

link_check.arxiv_title: Toward a Flexible Framework for Linear Representation Hypothesis Using Maximum Likelihood Estimation

extensions.title: Mapping Semantic & Syntactic Relationships with Geometric Rotation
extensions.evidence: Extends the LRH formalization of Park et al. (2024) to multilingual contexts and geodesic/manifold geometry, citing it as the foundational framework in Section 2.1.

link_check.arxiv_title: Mapping Semantic & Syntactic Relationships with Geometric Rotation

papers.search_notes: Searched arXiv (2311.03658), Semantic Scholar (265042984), ICML 2024 proceedings. Has 8 OpenAlex citations as lower bound; found several high-quality direct extensions.

papers.title: InferAligner: Inference-Time Alignment for Harmlessness through Cross-Model Guidance

extensions.title: Inference-time Alignment in Continuous Space
extensions.evidence: Cites InferAligner [50] as a key prior inference-time alignment method and proposes a continuous-space extension in Section 2, explicitly positioning its approach relative to InferAligner's cross-model safety-vector extraction.

papers.search_notes: Searched arXiv (2401.11206), ACL Anthology, OpenReview, GitHub (Jihuai-wpy/InferAligner). Has 10 OpenAlex citations. Found one confirmed methodological extension; other citations appear to be background mentions.

papers.title: Embedded Named Entity Recognition using Probing Classifiers
papers.search_notes: Searched arXiv (2403.11747), ACL Anthology, HuggingFace papers, Semantic Scholar. Has 2 OpenAlex citations as lower bound, but neither appears to extend the EMBER method specifically; one citation is a background mention. No qualifying follow-ups confirmed.

papers.title: Steering Knowledge Selection Behaviours in LLMs via SAE-Based Representation Engineering
papers.search_notes: Searched arXiv (2410.15999), ACL Anthology, NAACL 2025 proceedings, GitHub (yuzhaouoe), ResearchGate. Has 2 OpenAlex citations. Found one direct methodological extension via ResearchGate snippet. Link tentative — should be verified.

papers.title: Activation Steering for LLM Alignment via a Unified ODE-Based Framework
papers.search_notes: Searched OpenReview (ICLR 2026 forum id CFewUmgIIL), arXiv, Google Scholar. ICLR 2026 paper; too recent for follow-ups.

papers.title: Differentially Private Steering for Large Language Model Alignment
papers.search_notes: Searched arXiv (2501.18532), ICLR 2025 page, GitHub (UKPLab/iclr2025-psa), Google Scholar. Found arxiv 2505.08849 'Improved Algorithms for Differentially Private Language Model Alignment' but it focuses on DP-DPO/RLHF training, not activation-steering PSA; does not cite PSA, so excluded. No qualifying follow-ups confirmed.

papers.title: LEGEND: Leveraging Representation Engineering to Annotate Safety Margin for Preference Datasets
papers.search_notes: Searched arXiv (2406.08124), AAAI OJS, ResearchGate, alphaXiv. No confirmed qualifying follow-ups found that genuinely engage the LEGEND method.

papers.title: Beyond Linear Probes: Dynamic Safety Monitoring for Language Models
papers.search_notes: Searched OpenReview (ICLR 2026 forum id AGWa8whf92), arXiv, Google Scholar. ICLR 2026 paper; too recent for follow-ups.

papers.title: Are Sparse Autoencoders Useful? A Case Study in Sparse Probing

extensions.title: Rethinking Sparse Autoencoders: Select-and-Project for Fairness and Control from Encoder Features Alone
extensions.evidence: Directly cites Kantamneni et al. (2025) 'Are sparse autoencoders useful?' [15] as foundational motivation and proposes select-and-project as a response to its negative findings on SAE probe utility.

link_check.arxiv_title: Rethinking Sparse Autoencoders: Select-and-Project for Fairness and Control from Encoder Features Alone

extensions.title: Inference-Time Decomposition of Activations (ITDA): A Scalable Approach to Interpreting Large Language Models
extensions.evidence: Cites Kantamneni et al. (arXiv:2502.16681) as a key negative result benchmark and proposes ITDA as a scalable alternative that addresses the limitations identified in the sparse probing case study.

link_check.arxiv_title: Inference-Time Decomposition of Activations (ITDA): A Scalable Approach to Interpreting Large Language Models

papers.search_notes: Searched arXiv (2502.16681), ICML 2025 page, Google Scholar. Found two direct methodological extensions that explicitly cite and build on the paper's negative probing findings.
